questions.text: A disc, a hoop, and a sphere of the same mass and radius are rolled down from a frictionless, inclined plane. Which has a greater speed on reaching the ground?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The sphere has the greatest speed on reaching the ground.
The sphere has the greatest speed on reaching the ground. The shape of loop is as follows;

The sphere has the greatest speed on reaching the ground. This is because a sphere has the lowest moment of inertia of the three objects, which means that it will accelerate the most quickly when rolling down the inclined plane. The hoop has the highest moment of inertia, and the disc has an intermediate moment of inertia. Therefore, the sphere will have the greatest speed on reaching the ground, while the hoop will have the slowest speed.
The sphere has the greatest speed on reaching the ground.

questions.explanation: The sphere has most of its mass near the axis of rotation, so it will have the smallest moment of inertia and will reach the ground first.The disc has its mass uniformly distributed, so will have a greater moment of inertia and will arrive second.The hoop has all its mass concentrated away from the center, so will have the greatest moment of inertia. It will arrive last, but will then travel farther away on the horizontal plane than the other shapes, as it will have stored more of the original potential energy in the form of rotational energy.

questions.text: In rotational motion, angular momentum plays a role which is analogous to that played by _ in linear motion.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Linear velocity is analogous to rotational velocity in rotational motion.
Linear momentum is analogous to angular momentum in linear motion.
Linear acceleration is analogous to angular acceleration in rotational motion.
Inertia is analogous to mass.

questions.explanation: Angular momentum is completely analogous to linear momentum. It has the same implications in terms of carrying rotation forward, and it is conserved when the net external torque is zero. Angular momentum, like linear momentum, is also a property of the atoms and subatomic particles.

questions.text: The ratio of angular speed of moon around the Earth to its angular speed about its own axis is:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The moon orbits the Earth once every 27.322 days. In the same amount of time, the moon rotates once on its axis. As a result, the moon does not seem to be spinning but appears to observers from Earth to be keeping almost perfectly still (this is called synchronous rotation). So its angular speed (W) around its own axis is W =2π /T W(moon) = (2π)/(27.322 x 24 x 3600) = 2.67*10(-6) rad/s For orbital motion around the earth, the angular speed is W(earth) = (2π)/(27.322 x 24 x 3600) = 2.67*10(-6) rad/s So the ratio is 1:1

questions.text: The moment of inertia of a solid sphere of mass ‘m’ having radius ‘r’ about a line passing through its center is:

options.explanation: This option represents the moment of inertia of a solid sphere, which is incorrect. The correct formula for the moment of inertia of a solid sphere is (2/5)mr^2.
The moment of inertia of a solid sphere about a line passing through its center is given by the formula:I = (2/5)mr²where m is the mass of the sphere and r is its radius.
This formula represents the moment of inertia of a thin spherical shell, not a solid sphere. For a solid sphere, the correct formula is (2/5)mr^2.
This formula does not represent the moment of inertia of a solid sphere. It is not the correct formula for this case.
This formula represents the moment of inertia of a point mass, not a solid sphere. It is not the correct formula for calculating the moment of inertia of a solid sphere.

questions.explanation: The moment of inertia of a solid sphere about a line passing through its center is given by the formula:I = (2/5)mr²where m is the mass of the sphere and r is its radius.

questions.text: Law of conservation of momentum states that:I. there is no external force applied to a system, then the total momentum of that system remains constant II. if there is an external force applied to a system, then the total momentum of that system remains constant III. if there is no external force applied to a system, then the total momentum of that system keeps changing
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Right answer.The law of conservation of momentum states that, for two or more bodies in an isolated system acting upon each other, their total momentum remains constant unless an external force is applied. Therefore, momentum can neither be created nor destroyed and will stay constant.

Wrong answer.Law of conservation of momentum states that. For two or more bodies in an isolated system acting upon each other, their total momentum remains constant unless an external force is applied. Therefore, momentum can neither be created nor destroyed.

Wrong answer.Law of conservation of momentum states that. For two or more bodies in an isolated system acting upon each other, their total momentum remains constant unless an external force is applied. Therefore, momentum can neither be created nor destroyed.

Wrong answer.Law of conservation of momentum states that. For two or more bodies in an isolated system acting upon each other, their total momentum remains constant unless an external force is applied. Therefore, momentum can neither be created nor destroyed.

questions.explanation: If there is no external force applied to the system, then the total momentum of that system remains constant. If an external force is applied then the total momentum will change.The law of conservation of momentum states that, for two or more bodies in an isolated system acting upon each other, their total momentum remains constant unless an external force is applied. Therefore, momentum can neither be created nor destroyed and will stay constant.

questions.text: The earth rotates on its axis once a day. Suppose, by some process the earth contracts so that it’s radius is only half as large as at present, then how long the earth will take to complete it’s rotation?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per the formula given.
Incorrect as per the formula given.

Here we assume that the mass of the earth remains the same, the moment of the inertia of the earth rotating about its axis is : I=⅖ MR2-------eq1 The angular velocity of the earth is: ω=2pi/T-------eq 2 When the radius is reduced to half, The new moment of inertia is I=2/5MR2=2/5M(R/2)2----eq3 The new time period is T’ and the new angular velocity is: ω’=2pi/T’----eq4 Applying the law of conservation of angular momentum, we get: Iω=I’ω’ From eq 1, 2, 3, and 4 we get; (2/5MR2)*(2pi/T)=[2/5M(R/2)2]*(2pi/T’) ->T’=T/4 Since T=24 hrs, T=24/6=6 hrs.
Incorrect as per the formula given.

questions.explanation: Here we assume that the mass of the earth remains the same, the moment of the inertia of the earth rotating about its axis is : I=⅖ MR2-------eq1 The angular velocity of the earth is: ω=2pi/T-------eq 2 When the radius is reduced to half, The new moment of inertia is I=2/5MR2=2/5M(R/2)2----eq3 The new time period is T’ and the new angular velocity is: ω’=2pi/T’----eq4 Applying the law of conservation of angular momentum, we get: Iω=I’ω’ From eq 1, 2, 3, and 4 we get; (2/5MR2)*(2pi/T)=[2/5M(R/2)2]*(2pi/T’) ->T’=T/4 Since T=24 hrs, T=24/6=6 hrs.

questions.text: The rate of change of angular velocity with respect to _ defines angular acceleration.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Speed refers to the rate at which an object covers distance. It is a scalar quantity and is calculated as the distance traveled divided by the time taken. Speed does not directly relate to angular acceleration.

Frequency is the number of occurrences of a repeating event per unit of time. It is commonly used to describe how many cycles or oscillations occur in a given time interval. While frequency is related to angular velocity (as angular velocity is often expressed in radians per second), it does not define angular acceleration.
Distance is the total length traveled by an object along its path. It is a scalar quantity and is not directly related to angular acceleration.

Time is the duration or interval between two events. In the case of angular acceleration, it is the time with respect to which the rate of change of angular velocity is calculated. Angular acceleration is expressed in units like radians per second squared (rad/s^2) or degrees per second squared (°/s^2).

Gravity is a force that attracts objects with mass towards one another. While gravity can influence the motion of objects and the forces acting on them, it is not directly related to defining angular acceleration.

questions.explanation: The term rate of change is always used for time. This is because time can never be a constant. Its value goes on increasing.All options except D are incorrect because speed, frequency, distance and gravity are incorrect because angular velocity doesn't change with respect to them rather it changes due to time.

questions.text: A body having translatory motion possesses _ and _. In the same way a body having rotatory motion possesses _ and _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Angular momentum… Angular velocity…. Linear momentum….Linear velocity
options.explanation: Incorrect sequence.A motion in which all the points of a body moving uniformly in a single direction is called translational motion. When an object undergoes translational motion, there is no change in its orientation. In this motion, all the particles of the object move parallel to each other. So the objects moving with translatory motion possess linear properties.Rotatory motion is the motion that occurs when a body rotates on its own axis. A few examples of the rotatory motion are as follows: The motion of the earth about its own axis around the sun is an example of rotary motion. So the objects moving with rotatory motion possess angular properties

When an object undergoes translational motion, there is no change in its orientation. In this motion, all the particles of the object move parallel to each other. So the objects moving with translatory motion possess linear properties.Rotatory motion is the motion that occurs when a body rotates on its own axis. A few examples of the rotatory motion are as follows: The motion of the earth about its own axis around the sun is an example of rotary motion. So the objects moving with rotatory motion possess angular properties.

When an object undergoes translational motion, there is no change in its orientation. In this motion, all the particles of the object move parallel to each other. So the objects moving with translatory motion possess linear properties.Rotatory motion is the motion that occurs when a body rotates on its own axis. A few examples of the rotatory motion are as follows: The motion of the earth about its own axis around the sun is an example of rotary motion. So the objects moving with rotatory motion possess angular properties

options.text: Angular velocity…. Angular momentum….. Linear momentum….. Linear velocity

When an object undergoes translational motion, there is no change in its orientation. In this motion, all the particles of the object move parallel to each other. So the objects moving with translatory motion possess linear properties.Rotatory motion is the motion that occurs when a body rotates on its own axis. A few examples of the rotatory motion are as follows: The motion of the earth about its own axis around the sun is an example of rotary motion. So the objects moving with rotatory motion possess angular properties

questions.text: When brakes of a car are applied, the flywheel's angular velocity reduces from 900 cycles/min to 720 cycles/min 6 sec. Angular retardation is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: First, we must convert the given angular velocities from cycles per minute to radians per second. We know that 1 cycle is equal to 2π radians. Therefore,Angular velocity at the start = 900 cycles/min = (900 x 2π) / 60 rad/s = 30π rad/s Angular velocity at the end = 720 cycles/min = (720 x 2π) / 60 rad/s = 24π rad/sThe time taken for the flywheel to reduce its angular velocity from 30π rad/s to 24π rad/s is 6 seconds. We can use the formula for angular retardation:Angular retardation (α) = (ω2 - ω1) / tWhere ω2 is the final angular velocity, ω1 is the initial angular velocity, and t is the time taken.Substituting the given values, we get:Angular accelration (α) = (24π - 30π) / 6 = -π rad/s2Therefore, the angular retardation is π rad/s2
Incorrect as per the given formula.
Incorrect as per the given formula.
Incorrect as per the given formula.
Incorrect as per the given formula.

questions.text: A 800 kg car is turning round a corner at 6 m/sec as it travels along an arc of circle of radius 12 m. The magnitude of force exerted by pavement on the tyres to hold the car in circular path is _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: To calculate the magnitude of the force exerted by the pavement on the car's tires to hold it in a circular path, we need to consider the centripetal force acting on the car. The centripetal force is directed towards the center of the circular path and is given by the equation: Centripetal Force = (Mass x Velocity2) / Radius Given: Mass (m) = 800 kg Velocity (v) = 6 m/s Radius (r) = 12 m Plugging in the values, we have: Centripetal Force = (800 kg x (6 m/s)^2) / 12 m Centripetal Force = (800 kg x 36 m^2/s^2) / 12 m Centripetal Force = 2400 N Therefore, the magnitude of the force exerted by the pavement on the car's tires to hold it in a circular path is 2400 Newtons.
Incorrect as per the given formula.
Incorrect as per the given formula.
Incorrect as per the given formula.

questions.explanation: The formula for the centripetal force will be used here, which is: F=mv2/r Where m is the mass, v is the velocity and r is the radius. F=(800)(6)2/12 Solve the right-hand side of the equation to obtain: F=2400N

questions.text: What is angular velocity?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Change in angular rotation / change in time
options.explanation: It is correct as per the given definition.

options.explanation: Change in displacement/change in time is linear velocity.

options.explanation: Change in speed/change in time is linear acceleration.

questions.explanation: Angular velocity is the time rate at which an object rotates or revolves about an axis. Angular velocity is represented by the Greek letter omega (ω, sometimes Ω). It is measured in angle per unit time; hence, the SI unit of angular velocity is radians per second. Also;Angular velocity is the vector measure of the rotation rate, which refers to how fast an object rotates or revolves relative to another point. In simple words, angular velocity is the time rate at which an object rotates or revolves about an axis.

questions.text: Angular velocity cannot be
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Angular velocity can be zero. This simply means that the object is not rotating around the axis. For example, if a spinning top comes to a complete stop, its angular velocity would be zero.

Angular velocity can be negative. A negative angular velocity indicates that the object is rotating in the opposite direction. For example, if a spinning top starts rotating clockwise (positive angular velocity) and then reverses its direction to rotate counterclockwise (negative angular velocity), the angular velocity would be negative.

Angular velocity cannot be infinite. An infinite angular velocity would mean that the object is rotating infinitely fast, which is not physically possible. In reality, there are limits to how fast an object can rotate due to factors such as material strength and external forces.

The option "Linear" is not directly related to angular velocity. Linear motion refers to motion in a straight line, without any rotation involved. Angular velocity specifically describes the rate of rotation around an axis. The two concepts are distinct, and angular velocity is not measured in linear units like meters or kilometers.

questions.explanation: Angular velocity cannot be infinite. An infinite angular velocity would mean that the object is rotating infinitely fast, which is not physically possible. In reality, there are limits to how fast an object can rotate due to factors such as material strength and external forces.

questions.text: The highest moment of inertia if all the objects have same mass and same radius is of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: A ring about its axis perpendicular to the plane of the ring
options.explanation: The moment of inertia for a thin ring is given by I = mr2, where m is the mass and r is the radius. A ring with mass evenly distributed along its circumference has the highest moment of inertia among the options listed because all of its mass is located at the maximum distance (radius) from the axis. In summary, when all objects have the same mass and the same radius, the highest moment of inertia is associated with option a) "A ring about its axis perpendicular to the plane of the ring" because all of its mass is distributed at the maximum distance from the axis, resulting in the highest moment of inertia.

options.text: A solid sphere about one of its diameters
options.explanation: The moment of inertia for a solid sphere about one of its diameters is (2/5)mr2, where m is the mass and r is the radius. While this moment of inertia is substantial, it is not as high as that of the ring, as the mass in a solid sphere is distributed in a more concentrated manner toward the center.

options.text: A spherical shell about one of its diameters
options.explanation: The moment of inertia for a spherical shell about one of its diameters is (2/3)mr2, where m is the mass and r is the radius. This moment of inertia is less than that of the solid sphere and the ring, as the mass is concentrated at a slightly larger distance from the axis compared to the solid sphere.

options.text: A disc about its axis perpendicular to the plane of the disc
options.explanation: The moment of inertia for a disc about its axis perpendicular to the plane of the disc is (1/2)mr2, where m is the mass and r is the radius. This moment of inertia is the lowest among the options because the mass is concentrated closer to the axis of rotation compared to the other shapes.

questions.explanation: The moment of inertia for a thin ring is given by I = mr2, where m is the mass and r is the radius. A ring with mass evenly distributed along its circumference has the highest moment of inertia among the options listed because all of its mass is located at the maximum distance (radius) from the axis. In summary, when all objects have the same mass and the same radius, the highest moment of inertia is associated with option a) "A ring about its axis perpendicular to the plane of the ring" because all of its mass is distributed at the maximum distance from the axis, resulting in the highest moment of inertia.
hints: all the objects have same mass

questions.text: A meter stick is held vertically with one end on the floor and is then allowed to fall. The speed of the other end when it hits the floor, assuming that the end of the stick does not slip and g=9.8 m/s2, is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per the formula

This is the following solution: Let m and l be the mass and the length of the meter stick respectively then its center will be situated at l/2 the angular speed and m.l pf the stick is W and I respectively The M.I of the rod about an axis passing through its one end I=(1/3)Ml² =(1/3)m(1m)² =1/3m According to the conservation of energy, Ei= EfK. Ei+ P. Ei= K. Ef + P. EsIn this case,0+ mgl/2 = (1/2)Iw²+0w²=mgl/I W=square root(mgl/I) The speed of the stick is, V=1W V=1 square root (mgl/I) But L is =1 and 1=1/3m V=square root(mgl/(1/3)m) V=square root (3gl) Substitute all the values in the above equation; V=square root (3(9.81m/s²)×1m) V=5.42m/s Therefore, the speed of the other end of the stick just before it hits the floor is 5.42m/s.
Incorrect as per the formula
Incorrect as per the formula

Ei+ P. Ei= K. Ef + P. EsIn this case,0+ mgl/2 = (1/2)Iw²+0w²=mgl/I W=square root(mgl/I) The speed of the stick is, V=1W V=1 square root (mgl/I) But L is =1 and 1=1/3m V=square root(mgl/(1/3)m) V=square root (3gl) Substitute all the values in the above equation; V=square root (3(9.81m/s²)×1m) V=5.42m/s Therefore, the speed of the other end of the stick just before it hits the floor is 5.42m/s.

questions.text: The angular momentum of the particle rotating with a central force is constant due to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is not the reason for the constancy of angular momentum. A constant torque would cause a change in angular momentum according to Newton's second law for rotational motion (τ = dL/dt), unless the torque is zero.

A constant force is not the primary reason for the constancy of angular momentum. The constancy of angular momentum in a central force field is primarily related to the conservative nature of central forces.

While angular momentum is related to linear momentum, a constant linear momentum alone does not guarantee the constancy of angular momentum. The constancy of angular momentum is due to the specific nature of central forces.

This is the correct answer. In a central force field, the net torque acting on the particle is zero. Central forces act along the radial direction, and they don't produce a torque because they don't have a lever arm. Since τ = 0, according to the equation τ = dL/dt, the angular momentum L remains constant. In central force motion, the fact that the torque is zero is a fundamental reason for the constancy of angular momentum.

questions.explanation: This is the correct answer. In a central force field, the net torque acting on the particle is zero. Central forces act along the radial direction, and they don't produce torque because they don't have a lever arm. Since τ = 0, according to the equation τ = dL/dt, the angular momentum L remains constant. In central force motion, the fact that the torque is zero is a fundamental reason for the constancy of angular momentum.

questions.text: A body is moving in a circle at constant speed. Which statement is true?

options.text: The resultant force acts towards the centre of the circle
The resultant force acts away from the centre of the circle
None of these options are correct

questions.explanation: The resultant force, being the centripetal force, always acts towards the center of the circle in the situations where an object is moving in a circle, at a constant speed.

questions.text: The centripetal acceleration is maximum when radius r is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: None of these options are correct

questions.explanation: We know that centripetal acceleration (a) = v2/r where v is the linear speed and r is the radius. If v is constant, then a is inversely proportional to r. So, a will be maximum when r is decreasing.
